answer: Ramp offers a free tier for its platform [1][2], but the Developer API is a distinct feature set for server-to-server or long-lived workflows rather than a self-serve feature included by default in the free account tier [3][4][5]. Access to the Developer API typically involves OAuth 2.0 authentication [3][6] and may require specific administrative permissions [6]. While there is a public Developer MCP server for accessing documentation without authentication [7], production API access for your own applications generally requires a business account and potentially a support ticket for access provisioning [8].

results.title: Ramp Pricing Plans and Tiers Compared (2026) | CompareTiers
